memory.summary: I see the leisure option is tempting, but with the project due Thursday and barely started, I know I need to prioritize that over relaxing now.

desires: Not to let down the close friend group by seeming disengaged or late again
To avoid the immediate friction of pushing back on a teammate's idea even if it's slightly off
To ensure I don't feel exhausted tomorrow morning before the project deadline hits

goals.description: Eat a proper meal with the close friend group (cohort_3) to reinforce bonds and avoid feeling disconnected.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top value (0.5). Being disengaged from them would feel like a personal failure, so I must prioritize this connection now rather than working alone.

goals.description: Complete the quick_chore immediately to clear my mental space before the social event.
goals.rationale: My sleep discipline is high (8), and I don't want to carry stress into the evening. Finishing this small task gives me a sense of control without risking friction or awkwardness in conversations later.

goals.description: Draft a polite, non-confrontational message to teammate_A acknowledging their idea, deferring deep critique until we have more time together.
goals.rationale: I am conflict-avoidant. Pushing back right now risks ruining the harmony. Since the project deadline is soft and far away (Thursday), I can afford to let this sit for a few hours while I focus on people first.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing the trivial chore first clears mental clutter before I meet cohort_3, honoring my sleep discipline and need for control. It won't eat into our meal time since it's instant, ensuring I can fully engage with friends without lingering stress.
